# Edition Performance

> **Type:** Analytics
> **Provenance:** 1P
> **Contexts:** [event, finance, exhibitor, attendee]
> **Measures:** [event-edition-lifecycle]

## Purpose

Each AXPONA edition is a discrete project with its own P&L. Edition performance analytics measure the health of the overall business cycle — revenue growth, margin trend, and the balance between supply (exhibitors) and demand (attendees).

## Metrics

| Metric | Formula | Dimensions | Refresh |
|--------|---------|------------|---------|
| **edition_margin** | (edition_revenue - edition_cost) / edition_revenue | edition | per-edition |
| **revenue_growth_yoy** | (current_year - prior_year) / prior_year | edition | per-edition |
| **attendee_to_exhibitor_ratio** | attendee_count / exhibitor_count | edition | per-edition |
| **revenue_per_attendee** | total_revenue / attendee_count | edition | per-edition |
| **revenue_by_stream** | revenue per UFI category / total | edition, stream | per-edition |
| **cost_structure** | cost per category / total cost | edition, category | per-edition |

## Data Pipeline

```
Bronze                          Silver                          Gold
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Accounting data           ──▶   fct_edition_pnl              ──▶  v_edition_summary
(QuickBooks export)             dim_edition                        edition_trend_analysis
```

## Benchmarks

| Segment | Target | Signal |
|---------|--------|--------|
| EBITDA margin | >= 36% (2025 baseline) | Trending upward (from 13% in 2022) |
| Revenue growth | >= 10% YoY | Sustaining post-COVID momentum |
| Attendee:exhibitor ratio | 10-15:1 | Marketplace balance (11.5:1 in 2025) |
| Space revenue share | 60-70% | Healthy but watch for over-concentration |

## Capabilities

- edition-pnl-tracking
- revenue-stream-analysis
- yoy-trend-analysis

## Feedback Loop

- **Measured in:** Event + Finance
- **Consumed by:** Event (next-edition planning), Exhibitor (pricing strategy), all contexts (budget allocation)
- **Drives:** Edition-level investment decisions, pricing changes, cost management
- **Maturity path:** descriptive (current — OM P&L is already descriptive) → diagnostic (margin driver analysis)

## Related Patterns

- `event-edition-lifecycle` — the domain pattern being measured
